core_mechanism: a critical-threshold spontaneous-symmetry-breaking phase transition in an all-to-all (infinite-range) coupled ensemble, where uniform interactions mediated through a single shared collective mode drive the many units to self-organize into an ordered, symmetry-broken state once the coupling strength exceeds a critical value (a mean-field/superradiant-type collective bifurcation)
brief_reason: The abstract describes a phase transition driven by infinitely long-ranged (all-to-all) interactions induced via a single shared cavity mode, producing a self-organized state with a spontaneously broken spatial symmetry above a critical coupling boundary.
